compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Closing: addressed by merged reconciler fixes on main citing bug 9b94 — 9facfabc (converge unmappable assignees to unassigned instead of churning), 83a8922f (require EXACT assignee match, never fuzzy-assign), 333f137b (resolve normalized assignee variants e.g. joe-oakhart -> Joe Oakhart), merged via PR #28 (fix/assignee-unmappable-converge). The differ no longer re-emits an assignee update every pass for non-Jira-user assignees.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. Sync a rebar store to Jira (e.g. the REB cutover) where local tickets have
   assignees that are not Jira users (agent names like `claude`, `claude-opus`).
2. Run `rebar reconcile --mode dry-run` after the initial create pass.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** once a ticket is created+bound and its synced fields match, the
  reconciler emits no further mutations for it (convergence).
- **Actual:** ~323 of 355 bound tickets emit an `assignee` update every pass. The
  local assignee (e.g. `claude`) is not a Jira user, so the applier soft-fails the
  assign (`assignee 'claude' cannot be assigned — retrying without assignee`),
  Jira stays unassigned, and `_assignee_matches("claude", None)` is False — so the
  differ re-emits the same assignee update on the next pass. Perpetual churn (passes
  still succeed; the canary stays green — this is noise/inefficiency, not failure).

## Root cause

`outbound_differ._diff_fields` + `_assignee_matches`: a local assignee that cannot
be resolved to a Jira account never matches Jira's (unassigned) state, and there is
no "unresolvable assignee → don't re-emit" suppression after the applier soft-fails.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A bound ticket whose local assignee cannot be assigned in Jira does NOT
      re-emit an assignee update every pass (converge or suppress-after-soft-fail).
- [ ] Decide the intended semantics: skip assignee sync for non-Jira-user
      assignees, map agent identities to a service account, or record an
      unresolvable-assignee marker so the differ stops re-emitting.
- [ ] A dry-run after convergence shows 0 perpetual assignee-only updates.
compiled_state.title: Reconciler churns assignee updates for non-Jira-user assignees (never converges)
